title: Contributing to ihsMW
text: Thank interest contributing ihsMW! document outlines report bugs, suggest improvements, submit code.

title: Contributing to ihsMW
text: Please open issue GitHub Issues : short, descriptive title. output sessionInfo(). minimal reproducible example (reprex) demonstrating problem. expected vs. actual behaviour.

title: Contributing to ihsMW
text: harmonisation crosswalk lives inst/extdata/ihs_crosswalk.csv. correct mapping add new variable: Fork repository create branch (e.g., fix/rexp-cat01-mapping). Edit inst/extdata/ihs_crosswalk.csv directly. unsure mapping, set needs_review = TRUE. Open pull request describing variable changed, round, source (e.g., BID page number).

title: Contributing to ihsMW
text: Follow tidyverse style guide. Use cli::cli_abort(), cli::cli_warn(), cli::cli_inform() user-facing messages. Never use stop(), message(), cat(). Use rlang::abort() need low-level programmatic error. Use httr2 HTTP calls, httr curl.

title: Contributing to ihsMW
text: tests use httptest2 mocks run completely offline. API key required.
code: devtools::test()

title: Contributing to ihsMW
text: extend ihsMW new survey (e.g., IHPS future IHS6): Add round key IDNo .IHS_ROUNDS .IHS_IDNOS R/utils.R. Add weight/strata/cluster mappings .ihs_weight_vars R/IHS_survey.R. Extend inst/extdata/ihs_crosswalk.csv new ihs{N}_name column. Update mwi_surveys() relevant R file. Add corresponding tests update vignettes.

title: Getting started with ihsMW
text: ihsMW package maintains internal harmonisation crosswalk mapping thousands distinct metrics accurately tracking naming disparities across distinct rounds (IHS2 IHS5). can explicitly search map utilizing descriptive keywords: Alternatively, restrict query natively capturing data specifically explicit survey round implicitly avoiding unnecessary scope overlaps: wish review variables explicitly inside raw survey contexts, explore modules seamlessly natively leveraging World Bank infrastructure: Use ihs_label() natively referencing Stata attributes preserving explicit meaning behind discrete identifiers exactly mirroring internal documentation structures natively:
code: # Look up variables related to consumption ihs_search("consumption") # Find age-related variables specifically monitored during IHS5 ihs_search("age", round = "IHS5") # Look at all modules administered in IHS5 ihs_modules("IHS5") ihs_label("rexp_cat01")

title: Getting started with ihsMW
text: targeted harmonised variables identified, use overarching IHS() extractor capturing raw microdata formatting seamlessly binding values natively resolving dependencies securely across cache targets without rigid management requirements. Acquire standalone elements cleanly extracting structural representations iteratively locally: true power ihsMW extends gracefully targeting multiple variables pooled simultaneously across disjointed rounds without manual file joining restrictions matching parameters reliably natively: pooled structure integrates explicit ihs_round character string isolating origins intelligently supporting explicit group derivations cleanly binding data.frame layers exactly natively accurately. Caching Integration: ihsMW downloads raw components saving parameters directly onto persistent native disk locations ensuring data remains cached persistently seamlessly avoiding redundant API hits natively!
code: # Simple extraction targeted against IHS5 df_simple <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5") # Multi-round pooled extractions mapping harmonisations intelligently df_multi <- IHS(c("rexp_cat01", "hh_a02"), round = c("IHS4", "IHS5"))

title: Getting started with ihsMW
text: following blocks detail standard end--end integration mapping discovery consumption distributions smoothly integrating visualization bindings securely directly formatting outputs correctly.
code: library(ihsMW) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  # Find the consumption variable ihs_search("per capita consumption")  # Download IHS5 consumption data df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5")  # Quick summary df |> summarise(mean_cons = mean(rexp_cat01, na.rm = TRUE))  # Simple histogram ggplot(df, aes(x = rexp_cat01)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 50) +   labs(title = "Distribution of per capita consumption, Malawi IHS5")

title: Getting started with ihsMW
text: publishing research utilizing datasets harmonized accessed via ihsMW, must rigorously cite sampling procedures matching precise documents natively guaranteeing attribution accurately tracking standard rules. Always cite NSO Malawi World Bank LSMS natively recognizing precise structures smoothly linking contributions reliably! IHS5: National Statistical Office. Malawi - Fifth Integrated Household Survey 2019-2020. Ref: MWI_2019_IHS-V_v06_M. URL: https://microdata.worldbank.org/index.php/catalog/3818

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: IHS survey instruments undergo systematic redesigns rounds. policy goals evolve enumeration techniques improve, modules shuffled, variables renamed, categorical response codes completely restructured. instance, consider fundamental Consumption Aggregate. IHS2, primary real per capita consumption variable tracked rexp_cat011 natively within aggregates file. IHS4, fundamentally shifted, IHS5, merged definitions slightly demanding rigorous analyst verification. Similarly, Food Security Indicators like Food Insecurity Experience Scale (FIES) rigorously standardized later rounds. variable denoting whether household worried enough food labeled differently, placed inside fundamentally different module prefixes across IHS3 IHS5.

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: resolve inconsistencies, ihsMW package bundles static, manually curated crosswalk table. crosswalk securely maps single harmonised_name exact structural properties originally defined inside survey round. harmonised_name represents consistent variable identifier use querying ihsMW. Internally, package translates name round-specific identifiers natively intercepting HTTP downloads masking complexity. can inspect underlying crosswalk schema natively:
code: library(ihsMW)  # Load the raw crosswalk embedded inside the package cw <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "ihs_crosswalk.csv", package = "ihsMW")) head(cw)

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: executing analysis, critical determine desired indicators actually collected across target rounds. package exposes macro-level validation tracker summarizing crosswalk’s health efficiently: zero specific indicators, ihs_search() exposes exactly rounds recorded variable. can manipulate underlying tibble directly review coverage:
code: # Prints a report showing variable availability across rounds ihs_crosswalk_check() library(dplyr)  # Search for consumption and inspect coverage arrays ihs_search("consumption") |>    select(harmonised_name, ihs2_name, ihs3_name, ihs4_name, ihs5_name)

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: variables undergo semantic drift across rounds despite capturing fundamentally similar concepts. crosswalk flags needs_review = TRUE boolean internally. query indicator carrying flag, ihsMW emit non-blocking cli::cli_warn() advising manual intervention. flag signifies variable structurally aligns, underlying response options, specific definitions, unit structures fundamentally altered enumeration designers. writing academic papers, utilize flagged variable pooled across rounds, rigorously consult Basic Information Document (BID) explicitly outline methodological assumptions normalizing semantic differences data appendix.

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: harmonisation pipeline intercepts requests natively yielding pooled arrays iteratively appending tracking boundaries effortlessly.
code: # Extract consumption metrics mapped identically across IHS3, IHS4, and IHS5 df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = c("IHS3", "IHS4", "IHS5"))  # The output natively includes an `ihs_round` character tracking origins df |>   group_by(ihs_round) |>   summarise(mean_cons = mean(rexp_cat01, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: ihsMW binds datasets effectively, auto-harmonisation dangerous explicit conceptual alignment required compromised inherently: Variables fundamentally different concepts. module measured discrete 7-day recall IHS3, continuously tracked 14-day recall IHS4 without conversion mappings mathematically recorded. Value label changes. Region codes may completely renumbered (e.g., District 104 becoming District 201) breaking factor mappings silently numerical bindings. Recommendation: harmonisation wrapper substitute due diligence. Always meticulously review respective Basic Information Documents (BIDs) available World Bank Microdata Library ensuring conceptual endpoints align securely.

title: Cross-round harmonisation in ihsMW
text: internal crosswalk living document rigorously curated community. can suggest modifications, correct mapping errors, map previously unlinked variables across surveys contributing directly. Please submit mapping corrections explicitly tracking variable alignments GitHub issues tracker natively highlighting module origins cleanly.

title: Using survey weights with ihsMW
text: IHS utilizes stratified two-stage cluster sample design explicitly mapped represent national population, rural/urban disparities, specific geographical districts simultaneously. Unweighted means nationally representative. Computing simple average across unweighted data.frame yields biased estimates skewed disproportionate sampling allocations inherent rural versus urban tracks. “Overall, IHS5 sample design stratified two-stage sample… Therefore, imperative survey weights used making national-level regional-level estimates.” — National Statistical Office (2020), IHS5 Basic Information Document, Sampling Section.

title: Using survey weights with ihsMW
text: bridge gap raw data downloads proper statistical weights seamlessly, ihsMW provides IHS_survey() function. wrapper extracts intended indicators alongside underlying survey dimensions dynamically.
code: library(ihsMW)  # Automatically intercept consumption variables and inject structural weighting svy <- IHS_survey("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5")  # The output natively masks as a tbl_svy allowing tidy-eval manipulation class(svy) #> [1] "tbl_svy"     "svydesign2"  "svydesign"

title: Using survey weights with ihsMW
text: instantiated, survey object behaves exactly standard complex environment gracefully tracking variances internally. prefer classic structural base approximations using survey package: Alternatively, leverage srvyr package bridging dplyr pipelines weighted topologies intuitively:
code: library(survey)  # Compute the statistically accurate, nationally representative average svymean(~rexp_cat01, design = svy, na.rm = TRUE)  # Segment the nationally representative consumption by explicit strata svyby(~rexp_cat01, ~stratum, svy, svymean, na.rm = TRUE) library(srvyr)  # Tidy-style summaries mapping the underlying survey dimensions natively svy |>   group_by(stratum) |>   summarise(mean_cons = survey_mean(rexp_cat01, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Using survey weights with ihsMW
text: population sizes, spatial mapping coordinates, primary sampling units fundamentally shift globally cross-sectional spans, survey objects targeting discrete rounds intelligently pooled naively unified statistical architecture without intensive independent reweighting operations. Instead, querying multiple rounds natively, IHS_survey() protects inferences iteratively yielding distinct instantiated named list structure explicitly encapsulating unique configurations cleanly.
code: # Requesting pooled objects targets isolated arrays preserving isolated bounds svy_list <- IHS_survey("rexp_cat01", round = c("IHS4", "IHS5"))  # Apply functional iteration computing the unique representation safely lapply(svy_list, function(s) {   survey::svymean(~rexp_cat01, design = s, na.rm = TRUE) })

Crucial Warning: mappings assumed static always independently verified researcher. must actively cross-reference fields explicit World Bank Microdata Library BIDs natively avoiding invalid proxy mappings implicitly.

title: Using survey weights with ihsMW
text: Researchers approaching package frequently implement standard code workflows resulting structurally invalid endpoints natively: Using raw IHS() outcomes: Extracting variables exclusively natively bypassing survey targets avoids structural weights natively skewing estimates entirely inappropriately. Always utilize IHS_survey() inference depends . Naive pooling across bounds: Extracting IHS(round = "") static .dta equivalent independently wrapping natively single global .svydesign() fundamentally crashes cluster targets natively. Always retain separate round lists securely. Forgetting clustering attributes natively: Ignoring structural requirement utilizing standard survey::svydesign() directly without capturing nest = TRUE flag fundamentally masks variance clusters inflating assumed structural confidences artificially.

title: Access Malawi Integrated Household Survey Data in R
text: ihsMW package provides programmatic access Malawi Integrated Household Survey (IHS) microdata hosted World Bank. Much like WDI package elegantly bridges World Bank macroscopic development indicators R, ihsMW bridges complex, respondent-level household microdata endpoints. removes friction manual downloads, parsing, pooling, significantly accelerating research workflows.

code: # CRAN (coming soon) install.packages("ihsMW")  # Development version devtools::install_github("vituk123/ihsMW")

code: library(ihsMW) ihs_auth()                              # one-time setup ihs_search("consumption")              # find variables df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round="IHS5") # download data head(df)

title: Access Malawi Integrated Household Survey Data in R
text: Discovering targeted survey questions across thousands potential columns notoriously difficult due arbitrary module definitions. ihsMW, query intuitive indicators via built-search functions directly, retrieving exact metadata bounds including categorical label translations efficiently prior extracting raw dataset files. Researchers compiling cross-sectional trends often encounter fractured variable names spanning several disparate releases making chronological bindings intensely fragile. leveraging built-harmonisation engine recursively mapping embedded structures implicitly, querying across multiple rounds binds matrices securely bypassing fragmented architectures entirely ---box. Traditional unweighted aggregations mask significant spatial biases inherited rural stratifications invalidating macroscopic insights structurally. Pulling arrays iteratively utilizing dedicated survey weighting helpers builds tbl_svy instances capturing multi-tiered cluster architectures effortlessly.

text: MIT

title: Download Malawi IHS microdata — IHS
text: main interface ihsMW package. Downloads one IHS variables   across one survey rounds, applies cross-round harmonisation,   returns data requested format.

title: Download Malawi IHS microdata — IHS
code: IHS(   indicator,   round = "IHS5",   module = NULL,   return = c("data.frame", "list", "survey"),   format = c("parquet", "rds", "csv"),   cache = TRUE,   extra = FALSE )

title: Download Malawi IHS microdata — IHS
text: indicator Character vector harmonised variable names. Use ihs_search discover variable names. round Character vector IHS rounds include. One "IHS2", "IHS3", "IHS4", "IHS5", "". Note: IHS1 currently available via API. Default: "IHS5". module Optional character string restrict specific module. NULL (default), correct module determined automatically crosswalk. return Output format: "data.frame" (default), "list", "survey". format File format download caching: "parquet" (default), "rds", "csv". cache Logical. TRUE (default), use populate disk cache. extra Logical. FALSE (default), return requested indicator columns plus household ID columns. TRUE, include variables downloaded module (stratum, cluster, weights, etc.).

title: Download Malawi IHS microdata — IHS
text: return = "data.frame": single data.frame   ihs_round column.   return = "list": named list data.frames, one per round.   return = "survey": tbl_svy svydesign object.

title: Download Malawi IHS microdata — IHS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # One-time setup   ihs_auth()    # Download a single variable from the latest round   df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5")    # Multiple variables, multiple rounds   df <- IHS(c("rexp_cat01", "hh_a02"), round = c("IHS4", "IHS5"))    # All supported rounds   df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = "all")    # Return as a named list of data.frames   lst <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = c("IHS3", "IHS4", "IHS5"), return = "list")    # Include weights and design variables   df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5", extra = TRUE)    # Use rds format instead of parquet   df <- IHS("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5", format = "rds") } # }

title: Create a survey design object for Malawi IHS data — IHS_survey
text: Creates complex survey design object using survey srvyr packages. Automatically incorporates appropriate sampling weights, strata, clusters requested round enable statistically sound national estimations natively.

code: IHS_survey(indicator, round = "IHS5", ...)

title: Create a survey design object for Malawi IHS data — IHS_survey
text: indicator Character vector harmonised variable names. round single round string (e.g. "IHS5") "". ... Additional arguments passed IHS, module format.

title: Create a survey design object for Malawi IHS data — IHS_survey
text: tbl_svy object srvyr package installed,   otherwise svydesign object survey package. multiple   rounds requested, returns named list survey objects.

title: Create a survey design object for Malawi IHS data — IHS_survey
text: Survey weights differ across IHS rounds reflect complex sample design survey. Estimates produced using function representative national, urban/rural, regional, district level round independently. pool weights across rounds without consulting relevant Basic Information Document round. Cite sampling methodology: NSO Malawi (year), IHS[N] Basic Information Document. National Statistical Office, Zomba, Malawi.

title: Create a survey design object for Malawi IHS data — IHS_survey
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   svy <- IHS_survey("rexp_cat01", round = "IHS5")   survey::svymean(~rexp_cat01, design = svy)   svy |> srvyr::summarise(mean_cons = srvyr::survey_mean(rexp_cat01)) } # }

ℹ 96 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: n_rounds <dbl>, needs_review <lgl> ihs_search("expenditure", round = "IHS5") #> Found 1 variable matching "expenditure". #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>   harmonised_name label     module topic ihs2_name ihs3_name ihs4_name ihs5_name #>   <chr>           <chr>     <chr>  <chr> <chr>     <chr>     <chr>     <chr>     #> 1 hh_c22b         Expendit… f4     educ… NA        hh_c22b   hh_c22b   hh_c22b   #> # ℹ 2 more variables: n_rounds <dbl>, needs_review <lgl> ihs_search("age", fields = c("name", "label")) #> Found 272 variables matching "age". #> # A tibble: 272 × 10 #>    harmonised_name label    module topic ihs2_name ihs3_name ihs4_name ihs5_name #>    <chr>           <chr>    <chr>  <chr> <chr>     <chr>     <chr>     <chr>     #>  1 ac06a           Age at … f4     educ… ac06a     NA        NA        NA        #>  2 ac06b           Age at … f4     educ… ac06b     NA        NA        NA        #>  3 ac08            Did [na… f4     educ… ac08      NA        NA        NA        #>  4 ac08oth         A08 - O… f4     educ… ac08oth   NA        NA        NA        #>  5 adult           HH: Ind… f40    agri… adult     NA        NA        NA        #>  6 ag_b18          Do you … f31    agri… NA        ag_b18    NA        NA        #>  7 ag_b19          What is… f31    agri… NA        ag_b19    NA        NA        #>  8 ag_b19_os       What is… f31    agri… NA        ag_b19_os NA        NA        #>  9 ag_b214         During … f35    agri… NA        NA        ag_b214   ag_b214   #> 10 ag_d14          Garden … f37    agri… NA        ag_d14    ag_d14    ag_d14    #> # code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ihs_variables(round = "IHS4") ihs_variables(round = "IHS5", module = "hh_mod_g") } # }

title: ihsMW 0.1.0
text: IHS(): Download IHS microdata variable name, round, module. IHS_survey(): Return survey-design objects weighted analysis. ihs_auth(): One-time API key setup interactive browser-based guide. ihs_search(): Search variable names labels across rounds via crosswalk. ihs_variables(): Browse variables round via live NADA API. ihs_label(): Retrieve Stata variable labels crosswalk NADA. ihs_modules(): List available data modules per round. ihs_crosswalk_check(): Assess cross-round variable comparability. ihs_cache_info() ihs_cache_clear(): Manage local data cache. mwi_surveys(): Overview supported planned Malawi surveys.

title: ihsMW 0.1.0
text: IHS2 (2004/05) IHS5 (2019/20) fully supported via API. IHS1 (1997/98) yet available via World Bank Microdata Library.

title: ihsMW 0.1.0
text: data downloaded directly World Bank Microdata Library. free account API key required. See ?ihs_auth.
